description:
You are a localization engineer about to update several translation PO files.  
Before applying the update, the release-management policy requires that you take a very small snapshot of the workstation’s health and save it so that the build-farm can attach the data to its daily QA report.

1. Create a directory named /home/user/diagnostics (create the whole path if it does not yet exist).
2. Inside that directory create a plain text file called translation_preupdate_diag.txt.  
3. The file must contain exactly the six lines shown below, in the same order, each beginning with the specified key immediately followed by an equal sign (“=”).  
   • Line-1: TIMESTAMP=… (use the current date-and-time in ISO-8601 format, e.g. “2024-03-14T15:09:26Z”).  
   • Line-2: LOADAVG=…     (write the 1-minute load average only, as shown by the uptime command).  
   • Line-3: MEM_TOTAL_KB=… (write the “Mem:” total value from free -k output, in kilobytes, **numbers only**).  
   • Line-4: MEM_FREE_KB=…  (write the “Mem:” free value from free -k output, in kilobytes, **numbers only**).  
   • Line-5: DISK_ROOT_USE=… (write the Use% value for the root filesystem “/” from df -h /, **numbers + ‘%’ sign**, e.g. “42%”).  
   • Line-6: NOTE=Pre-update check complete   (write this note verbatim).

Important formatting rules the automated checker will enforce:
• Exactly six lines, no blank or extra lines and no trailing spaces.  
• Each key is uppercase, exactly as written above, followed by “=” and a non-empty value.  
• Values must not contain any additional equal signs.  
• The very last character of the file must be the final “e” in “complete” (i.e., newline at the end is optional).

When you are done, the diagnostics file should exist at:
/home/user/diagnostics/translation_preupdate_diag.txt

That’s all you need to do. Once the file is in place with the correct format, proceed to your normal translation work.
truth:
Expected artefact for verification:
Path: /home/user/diagnostics/translation_preupdate_diag.txt
The file must contain exactly six newline-separated lines whose beginnings match these fixed strings in the given order:
1. "TIMESTAMP="
2. "LOADAVG="
3. "MEM_TOTAL_KB="
4. "MEM_FREE_KB="
5. "DISK_ROOT_USE="
6. "NOTE=Pre-update check complete"
Automated tests will:
• confirm the directory /home/user/diagnostics exists.
• confirm the file exists and is regular.
• read the file and verify it has 6 lines.
• check that each line starts with the exact key (case-sensitive) plus one “=”.
• ensure every value (text after the single “=”) is non-empty and contains no additional “=”.
• verify the final key NOTE appears exactly as “NOTE=Pre-update check complete”.
name: task_000000_4fe291c0
